title: poa strigosa subsp. anceps
intro: Poa strigosa subsp. anceps, also known as Poa anceps, is a subspecies of grass in the family Poaceae. It is native to New Zealand and Australia. The medicinal properties of Poa strigosa subsp. anceps lie in its ability to reduce inflammation and relieve pain, which is attributed to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones. This plant has been used to prepare herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions to alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as to soothe digestive problems.
